task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS
TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

input.content:
Project Health Briefing — Project Helios / Meridian Group
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-11

Executive Summary
Project Helios is currently assessed as ON TRACK with material risks that require active management. Core foundation work is complete, including requirements, architecture, API gateway implementation, development environment setup, and data schema finalization. However, several schedule-sensitive items remain in progress or overdue, and the July 25 Meridian product demo is a critical near-term milestone.

The most significant risks are Meridian source data quality issues, unresolved GeoSync licensing, dashboard and ingestion performance, UAT timing pressure, and dependency on Meridian IT environment access. If UAT slips by more than two weeks, the October 1 launch target may move into Q4 and trigger the contract Section 7.1 penalty review.

Current Progress
Overall status: ON TRACK, with minor-to-moderate delivery risk.

Completed items:
- Requirements documentation v1.0 completed.
- System architecture design completed.
- API gateway implementation completed and delivered.
- Development environment setup completed.
- Data schema finalization completed.
- Authentication QA test suite is fully passing: 4/4 tests passed.

Milestone/component progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete; target completion 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard: 60% complete; target completion 2026-07-22.
- Integration testing: 40% complete; integration test sign-off target 2026-07-25.

Q2/Q3 transition items still open:
- Data ingestion module remains in progress.
- Client dashboard v1 remains in progress.
- Integration test suite setup is overdue; original due date 2026-06-30, rescheduled to 2026-07-20.
- UAT environment provisioning is overdue; originally due 2026-07-01 and dependent on Meridian IT access.
- Performance benchmarking report is overdue; original due date 2026-07-05, owner Derek Okafor.

Testing status from Sprint 14:
- 10 of 15 test cases executed.
- 2 open failures; 3 test cases not yet run.
- Data pipeline batch ingestion: 5/7 passed.
- Dashboard testing: 1/4 complete.
- Open high-severity defects:
  - HEL-D-014: Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format. This affects approximately 40% of Meridian source data and is blocked pending Meridian sample data due 2026-07-15.
  - HEL-D-015: Large file ingestion above approximately 200k records causes memory overflow; root cause investigation is ongoing.

Key Risks
1. Meridian source data quality issues
- Likelihood: High; Impact: High; Risk score: 9.
- Current issue: Inconsistent date formats across Meridian data sources, including ISO 8601 and MM/DD/YYYY.
- Operational impact: Blocks defect HEL-D-014 resolution and affects a significant portion of source data.
- Current mitigation: Meridian committed to provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.

2. GeoSync license renewal risk
- Likelihood: Medium; Impact: High; Risk score: 6.
- Current issue: Third-party GeoSync v2.1 license renewal is pending legal review.
- Operational impact: Must be resolved before the July 25 demo.
- Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

3. Dashboard and ingestion performance risk
- Likelihood: Medium; Impact: High; Risk score: 6.
- Current issues:
  - Dashboard load-time benchmark for 100k records has not yet run.
  - Performance benchmark task HEL-247 has not started.
  - Large file ingestion fails at approximately 210k records due to memory overflow.
- Q3 target: Dashboard load time below 2 seconds for 100k records; ingestion throughput of 500k records/min sustained.

4. UAT timeline slippage
- Likelihood: High; Impact: Medium; Risk score: 6.
- Current issue: UAT period has already been extended by one week and is now scheduled for August 17-28.
- Strategic impact: Slippage of more than two weeks could push the October 1 target launch into Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review.

5. Meridian IT environment access delays
- Likelihood: Medium; Impact: Medium; Risk score: 4.
- Current issue: NovaBridge is awaiting Meridian IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup.
- Due date from Meridian: 2026-07-12.
- UAT access commitment: Meridian IT expects to provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

6. Key person dependency
- Likelihood: Low; Impact: High; Risk score: 3.
- Current issue: Derek Okafor is a key dependency, with no backup technical lead identified.
- Impact area: Performance benchmarking, technical leadership, and demo readiness.

7. Scope creep risk
- Likelihood: Low; Impact: Medium; Risk score: 2.
- Current issue: Three informal Meridian feature requests have been logged but not escalated.
- Related proposed Q3 feature additions remain subject to change-control approval.

Upcoming Q3 Commitments
July commitments:
- Meridian IT infrastructure diagram due from Meridian by 2026-07-12.
- GeoSync license renewal targeted for resolution by 2026-07-14.
- Clean Meridian sample dataset due by 2026-07-15.
- Data ingestion module completion target: 2026-07-18.
- Draft demo agenda due to Meridian by 2026-07-18.
- UAT environment access expected from Meridian IT by 2026-07-19.
- Integration test suite setup rescheduled target: 2026-07-20.
- Client dashboard v1 completion target: 2026-07-22.
- Product demo for Meridian stakeholders: 2026-07-25.
- Integration test sign-off target: 2026-07-25.

Product demo commitments for July 25:
- Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data.
- End-to-end pipeline walkthrough.
- Dashboard visualization with real data, per contract Section 9.3.
- Meridian sponsor specifically emphasized that the demo must use live data ingestion rather than mock data.

August commitments:
- UAT with Meridian Group: 2026-08-17 to 2026-08-28.
- Deploy to UAT environment.
- Support Meridian user testing sessions.
- Address UAT feedback, with two sprints budgeted.

August-September commitments:
- Performance hardening.
- Dashboard load time target: under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Ingestion throughput benchmarking target: 500k records/min sustained.
- External security penetration test; vendor still to be determined.

September commitments:
- Production environment setup.
- Runbook documentation.
- Hypercare plan finalization for a 30-day post-launch model.
- Final client training sessions.

Launch target:
- Target launch date remains 2026-10-01.

Proposed Q3 feature additions requiring change-control approval:
- Scheduled ingestion jobs using cron-based triggers.
- Email alerting for pipeline failures.
- Self-service mapping rule editor, deferred from Q2.

Issues Requiring Leadership Attention
1. Ensure executive focus on demo readiness
The July 25 demo is a major client-facing milestone. Leadership should ensure the team has the necessary priority and support to resolve the data format blocker, GeoSync license issue, and performance concerns before the demo.

2. Accelerate GeoSync licensing closure
GeoSync renewal is due for resolution by 2026-07-14. Because the dependency is under legal review and has high demo impact, leadership may need to reinforce urgency with Legal and the responsible owner.

3. Reinforce Meridian dependency management
Several critical items depend on Meridian: clean sample dataset, IT infrastructure diagram, and UAT environment access. Leadership should be prepared to escalate with Meridian leadership if the July 12, July 15, or July 19 commitments slip.

4. Address performance and stability risk immediately
The ingestion memory overflow defect and unstarted performance benchmarking create risk for both the demo and Q3 hardening goals. Leadership should verify that engineering capacity is allocated to HEL-D-015 and HEL-247 now, not after dashboard completion.

5. Protect the UAT schedule
The UAT window has already consumed a one-week extension. Leadership should treat any additional slippage as a launch-impacting issue and monitor the August 17 start date closely.

6. Reduce key-person dependency
Derek Okafor is a key technical dependency with no identified backup. Leadership should consider assigning a deputy or backup technical lead for performance, demo readiness, and technical decision continuity.

7. Enforce change control for Q3 feature additions
Proposed Q3 additions should not be accepted informally. Leadership should require formal change-control approval, especially given existing UAT and launch-date sensitivity.

Recommended Leadership Actions
- Confirm GeoSync license renewal status by 2026-07-14 and escalate if unresolved.
- Confirm Meridian delivery of the IT infrastructure diagram by 2026-07-12 and clean sample data by 2026-07-15.
- Request a focused demo-readiness review covering live ingestion, dashboard visualization, performance, and open defects.
- Require an owner-level recovery plan for overdue performance benchmarking and the large-file ingestion failure.
- Assign a backup technical lead or named deputy to reduce delivery risk.
- Maintain strict change-control discipline for any new Meridian feature requests.

Bottom Line
Project Helios remains broadly on track for the October 1 launch, but the delivery path is tight. The next two weeks are critical. Successful resolution of Meridian data issues, GeoSync licensing, performance defects, and environment access will determine whether the team can deliver the July 25 demo and protect the Q3 timeline.
